source_jobs.title: Join the CloudZero Collective — Our Talent Community
source_jobs.description:
Join the CloudZero Collective!

At CloudZero, we’re always on the lookout for curious, driven, and passionate people — even if the perfect role isn’t open just yet.



That’s why we’ve launched the CloudZero Collective: our talent community for future teammates who are excited about what we’re building in the world of cloud cost intelligence.



By submitting your resume here, you’ll:

• Be considered for future openings that align with your background as they become available.

• Stay top of mind as our team grows.

• Show your interest in joining a company shaping the future of cloud cost intelligence.



This community gives future candidates a way to stay informed about new roles, team updates, and life at CloudZero. It’s also a great place for referrals — if you know someone amazing who’d be a great fit for us down the line, send them our way!



Let’s grow our network with people excited to build the future.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive - West
source_jobs.description:
About the Role
We’re looking for an Account Executive who thrives on winning new business and driving impact. In this role, you’ll own the full sales cycle from prospecting to close, while helping scale a proven go-to-market motion in a high-growth SaaS company.

You’ll join a lean, high-performing sales team where every deal matters and individual contributions are highly visible. This is a great opportunity to run your territory with strong support from leadership, marketing, product, customer success, and our alliance partners. For ambitious sales professionals, this role offers more than just hitting quota, it’s a chance to grow your craft, influence how we sell, and be rewarded for impact.


What You’ll Do:

 - Own and close new business: Drive the full sales cycle from discovery and POC to close, partnering closely with Sales Engineers.

 - Hunt strategically: Build and execute territory plans, self-source opportunities, and collaborate with marketing and alliances to generate pipeline.

 - Run strong sales conversations: Lead effective Zoom and onsite meetings using frameworks like Sandler and Force Management.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner with product, marketing, customer success, and engineering to deliver solutions that resonate with customers.

 - Forecast responsibly: Manage pipeline using MEDDPICC and contribute to accurate, data-informed forecasting.

 - Leverage partnerships: Work alongside AWS and alliance sales teams to accelerate deals and expand reach.

 - Help us scale: Contribute to improving sales processes and playbooks as the team grows.

 - Stay curious: Continue learning about cloud, AWS, and DevOps to understand customer needs and industry trends.

What You’ll Bring:

 - 3+ years of closing experience in SaaS, ideally within cloud, DevOps, or adjacent technical spaces.

 - Hunter mindset: Demonstrated success generating and closing new business.

 - Sales fundamentals: Experience with structured sales methodologies (e.g., Sandler, MEDDPICC, Force Management).

 - Team-first approach: Comfortable collaborating across functions and learning from feedback.

 - Strong communication skills: Confident engaging both technical and business stakeholders.

 - Tech comfort: Familiarity with AWS/DevOps concepts and tools like Salesforce, Zoom, G-Suite/Office, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

 - Growth mindset: Motivated by fast-paced environments and excited to level up as the business scales.

Why Join Us:

 - High visibility & real impact: A lean sales team where your contributions are noticed and valued.

 - Greenfield opportunity: Large addressable market with room to build and grow new customer relationships.

 - Upside potential: Competitive compensation with uncapped OTE, aligned to performance.

 - Cross-functional exposure: Work closely with product, marketing, customer success, and engineering. Your voice and ideas matter.

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Product Manager, Agentic Practice
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is seeking a Senior/Staff Product Manager, Agentic Practice to own and evolve a core product area within our FinOps and cloud cost intelligence platform. This role is equally balanced between technical depth and customer focus, and is ideal for a PM who built credibility in another discipline - such as engineering, finance, DevOps, product marketing, sales engineering, or TAM - before moving into product management.

You are AI-native in how you work. You use coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, or similar) as part of your everyday practice — translating your own requirements into working prototypes, accelerating discovery cycles, and proving out ideas before engineering invests. You continually refine your own patterns and tools for using AI to do PM work better.

You will operate in an enterprise, direct sales environment, working closely with engineering, experience design, sales, marketing, and customer teams. Success in this role requires strong product judgment, technical fluency, and an exceptional ability to gather and synthesize market intelligence from both customers and non-customer potential users.

This role is ideal for a PM who is uncomfortable shipping features without understanding whether and why customers will use them.

 
 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Product Strategy & Market Discovery

 - Own the product vision, strategy, and multi-quarter roadmap for a core CloudZero product area

 - Identify high-impact problems at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, cost, usage, and business value

 - Actively collect market intelligence from:
   
   - Existing enterprise customers
   
   - Prospects and churned accounts
   
   - Industry practitioners (FinOps, DevOps, Finance, Engineering)
   
   - Analysts, communities, events, and internal subject-matter experts

 - Translate market signals into clear product hypotheses, define what success or failure looks like, and actively revisit decisions based on real-world usage and feedback

 - Define and track success metrics (OKRs, KPIs) tied to customer value and revenue impact

Execution & Delivery

 - Lead full-stack product development from discovery through delivery and iteration

 - Write clear, actionable product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria

 - Build interactive prototypes from your own requirements using coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, or similar) to validate intent and gather feedback before engineering commits

 - Partner closely with engineering to make sound technical and architectural tradeoffs

 - Collaborate deeply with our experience design team to deliver intuitive, scalable, and trustworthy UI experiences

 - Ship iteratively with a strong bias toward learning and post-launch iteration, including revisiting, refining, or deprecating features based on adoption and customer impact

 - Make informed tradeoffs and ship under time pressure, balancing short-term GTM needs with long-term product quality and platform health

Enterprise Customer & Go-to-Market Enablement

 - Actively support pre-sales activities, including enterprise demos, customer calls, roadmap discussions, and RFP responses

 - Partner with sales engineering and product marketing to shape positioning, messaging, and enablement materials

 - Engage directly with customers post-sale to validate solutions, gather feedback, and drive adoption

 - Serve as a subject-matter expert for strategic accounts and internal stakeholders

Cross-Functional Leadership

 - Act as the connective tissue between engineering, design, sales, marketing, post-sales and customer success teams

 - Clearly communicate priorities, decisions, and tradeoffs across technical and non-technical audiences

 - Influence without authority and align teams around shared outcomes

 - Mentor junior PMs or cross-functional partners when appropriate

 
 


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–4+ years of experience as a Product Manager on a B2B SaaS product

 - Proven expertise in

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is growing fast. Our customer base is expanding, the data challenges we're solving are getting more complex, and the core platform team is scaling to match. As a Senior Software Engineer on the Droptable team (Billing Ingest), you'll own the architecture at the heart of our product: the backend data processing, storage, and query infrastructure that powers everything our customers depend on.

This is actual platform engineering work at actual scale, not a maintenance role or a backlog triage job. You'll design the data models, pipelines, and query systems that let us onboard increasingly large customers with increasingly complex analytical needs, and you'll work directly with engineers and business stakeholders to build features that answer questions no one has thought to ask yet.

If you thrive on hard data problems, care deeply about performance and scalability, and want to see your work matter to customers in direct and measurable ways, this role was built for you.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and optimize scalable data models, queries, and storage solutions to improve performance and efficiency across the platform

 - Maintain and enhance a custom-built query engine, ensuring flexibility and optimal performance as customer needs evolve

 - Build for scale. Onboarding customers with larger datasets and increasingly complex analytical requirements is a core part of the job

 - Collaborate with engineers and business stakeholders to develop data-driven features that solve real customer problems

 - Continuously improve engineering processes and data infrastructure to enable rapid development and iteration


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Proficiency in SQL and hands-on experience with cloud-based databases and data storage solutions (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery)

 - Proficiency in Python

 - Hands-on experience building and optimizing ETL pipelines and frameworks

 - Deep understanding of data modeling principles and data warehouse design for analytics

 - Strong problem-solving and debugging skills in large-scale data environments

 - Ability to communicate complex data concepts clearly to business stakeholders and cross-functional teams


BONUS IF YOU HAVE...

 - Experience with Snowflake data stores, AWS Step Functions, and AWS Lambda

 - Exposure to machine learning pipelines and model deployment in data environments


ABOUT CLOUDZERO

Cloud cost management is one of the biggest challenges organizations face today. As cloud adoption continues to accelerate, so do the complexities and costs associated with it, and macroeconomic conditions only increase pressure to prove cloud efficiency.

CloudZero is a SaaS platform at the intersection of next-generation cloud cost management and FinOps. We ingest billing and usage data from all cloud, SaaS, and PaaS providers, organize it in real time according to our customers' business structures, and empower organizations to make more informed business decisions.

Since our founding in 2016, our mission has been to make efficient innovation a reality for every cloud-driven organization. We believe every engineering decision is a buying decision, and we're applying proven reliability engineering principles to financial efficiency.

We believe the best AI empowers users with clear insights and confident decisions, transforming complex cloud cost data into actionable intelligence that drives meaningful business outcomes.

To date, we've raised over $56 million from leading venture capital firms. We're solving problems of massive scale, business importance, and complexity in a space that needs it more than ever.


EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

CloudZero is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. All job offers are contingent upon the candidate passing background and refe

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, Agentic Systems
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is growing fast. Our customer base is expanding, the data challenges we're solving are getting more complex, and the platform is scaling to match. As a Principal Product Manager for Agentic Systems, you'll own some of the hardest product problems at CloudZero: translating noisy financial and operational signals into trusted, explainable, and safe recommendations or actions that engineers and finance teams can act on with confidence.

This is real systems product work at scale, not a stakeholder management role or a roadmap shuffling job. You'll work at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, data orchestration, automation, and human-in-the-loop decision-making, shaping how CloudZero evolves from insight delivery toward intelligent action. You'll define when a system should observe, recommend, require approval, or act autonomously, and you'll build the trust frameworks that make that spectrum safe and usable.

This role is ideal for a PM who thrives on deep systems thinking, prefers owning one complex problem end-to-end over managing teams, and measures career growth by problem difficulty and impact, not org size.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Agentic Product Strategy & Problem Definition

 - Own the vision, strategy, and multi-quarter roadmap for CloudZero's agentic and automated decision systems

 - Identify high-impact opportunities to translate financial signals (spend anomalies, budgets, forecasts, unit economics) and operational signals (usage, performance, configuration) into clear recommendations or automated actions

 - Define when systems should observe, recommend, require human approval, or act autonomously

 - Explicitly model trust, safety, blast radius, rollback, and auditability in all designs

 - Partner with customers, prospects, and internal experts (FinOps, Engineering, Finance) to validate where automation creates value and where it creates risk

Systems Design & Execution

 - Lead end-to-end product development for complex, system-level workflows

 - Write clear, precise product requirements and specifications for signal ingestion and normalization, prioritization and decision logic, human-in-the-loop controls, and explanations, confidence signals, and guardrails

 - Partner deeply with engineering on architectural and technical tradeoffs, balancing correctness, latency, scalability, and user trust

 - Collaborate with experience design to ensure agentic behavior is understandable, predictable, and usable, not opaque or magical

 - Drive iterative delivery with explicit success criteria, learning plans, and rollback strategies

Customer Trust, Validation & Enablement

 - Engage directly with enterprise customers to validate where automation is welcomed, where explainability is required, and where human approval is non-negotiable

 - Support pre-sales and post-sales conversations around intelligent automation, including demos, roadmap discussions, and risk assessments

 - Partner with product marketing and sales engineering to clearly articulate what the system does, what it does not do, and why users should trust it

 - Act as the internal subject-matter expert on CloudZero's automation and agentic capabilities for both internal teams and strategic customers

Cross-Functional Leadership

 - Serve as the connective tissue between platform engineering, data engineering, design, security, and customer-facing teams

 - Communicate complex system behavior and tradeoffs clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Influence without authority; align teams around shared outcomes, not just shipped features

 - Mentor peers and cross-functional partners through technical product decision-making


WHAT YOU BRING

 - 5–8+ years of experience as a Product Manager building B2B SaaS products in technically complex domains

 - Strong product craft, including writing detailed PRDs, specs, and decision documents, and translating ambiguous problems into well-scoped, testable solutions

 - Experie

source_jobs.title: Distinguished Engineer, Data Platform
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is growing fast. Our customer base is expanding, the data challenges we're solving are getting more complex, and the platform is scaling to match. As a Distinguished Engineer on the Data Engineering team, you'll own some of the hardest infrastructure problems at CloudZero: shaping the next-generation streaming data platform, the dimensional cost model underlying every attribution decision, the hot/cold storage architecture serving both real-time and historical queries, and the query engine that powers our entire product.

This is real platform architecture work at real scale, not a consulting role or a review-and-advise job. You'll define the roadmap, drive the foundational decisions, and be a force multiplier for a talented engineering team — evolving CloudZero from batch-oriented pipelines toward a streaming-first architecture where cost attribution reaches engineers within seconds of a resource being used, not the next morning.

This role is ideal for an architect who has built systems like this before, has the scars to prove it, and wants to see their decisions matter in direct and measurable ways for customers and for the business.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Define the Data Platform Architecture

 - Lead end-to-end technical design for CloudZero's next-generation data platform, from event ingestion and stream processing through hot/cold storage and the query layer to the API surface

 - Document architectural decisions, tradeoffs, and migration strategies with the rigor of an RFC-driven process

 - Shape and drive every layer of the new architecture: event ingestion, stream processing and enrichment, real-time serving, analytical storage, query layer, and API

Drive Streaming Infrastructure to Production

 - Design and deliver CloudZero's real-time data pipeline from ingestion through enrichment to serving

 - Establish SLOs for throughput, latency, and correctness, and build the operational playbooks that make this system trustworthy enough to replace the batch pipelines our entire product currently depends on

 - Tackle real-time streaming at scale across thousands of customers simultaneously, with fault tolerance, backpressure awareness, and correctness as non-negotiables

Tackle the Dimension Cardinality Problem

 - Redesign CloudZero's dimensional cost model to support high-cardinality, multi-dimensional cost attribution without runaway materialization costs

 - Drive incremental, delta-based materialization strategies using modern open table formats, dramatically reducing expensive full-rebuild jobs and unlocking millions in annual infrastructure savings

Evolve the Query Layer

 - Assess CloudZero's current query infrastructure, drive in-flight migrations to completion, and lead the evolution of the query engine layer going forward

 - Own performance optimization across partition pruning, predicate pushdown, and query planning, and set the vision for how the query layer grows as data volumes scale 10x

Extend Cost Attribution to Real-Time

 - Evolve CloudZero's proprietary cost attribution engine from a batch-oriented model to one that assigns complex cost dimensions by team, feature, and customer within seconds of resource usage

 - Rethink enrichment, data lineage, and correctness guarantees in a streaming context

Shape the Data Engineering Roadmap

 - Partner with product, infrastructure, and analytics engineering to define a multi-year data platform roadmap

 - Build consensus across engineering leadership on foundational investments including table formats, streaming frameworks, query engines, and schema management

Elevate the Engineering Team

 - Participate in architecture reviews, contribute to design patterns and best practices, and mentor senior and staff engineers through code review, pairing, and structured feedback

 - Make everyone around you better, not by directing, but by raising the collective craft


WHAT YOU BRING

Data Platform & Architecture

 - 10+ years in data engineerin

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager, Performance & Social
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Growth Marketing Manager to own performance marketing, social channels, and to drive the experimentation that turns them into qualified pipeline and earned visibility. This is a hands-on role for a data-first operator who treats every program as an experiment, lets the numbers decide what scales, and has the track record to back it up.

You'll own paid performance across search and social, run our organic and paid social channels, and drive PR in close collaboration with Content Marketing. You'll work alongside a team of Growth, Brand, and Content marketers, sharing learnings and compounding what works across the function.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


PERFORMANCE MARKETING (CORE MANDATE)

 - Own paid performance across search and social, from strategy through daily execution and optimization

 - Manage spend against pipeline and efficiency targets, and make the allocation calls

 - Own web conversion rate optimization across landing pages and on-site experience

 - Drive backlinking and other earned-visibility strategies to build domain authority and organic reach

 - Support the ABM program with personalized targeting and experimental optimization

 - Run audience, creative, and spend testing, and scale what earns it

 - Own performance analytics across funnels and attribution


SOCIAL CHANNELS (OWNED)

 - Own organic social across the company page and executive amplification

 - Manage day-to-day execution of paid and organic social, with a clear point of view on reach, engagement, and conversion

 - Translate social engagement into measurable pipeline contribution


PUBLIC RELATIONS (WITH CONTENT MARKETING)

 - Collaborate with Content Marketing to run the PR program in house, keeping coverage moving and on message

 - Place press releases, announcements, and reactive news opportunities across product launches and company milestones

 - Amplify earned coverage across social and owned channels to extend its reach

 - Track PR performance and tie coverage back to awareness and pipeline where possible


ANALYTICS & REPORTING

 - Track and report on performance, social, and PR, including spend efficiency, conversion, coverage, and overall pipeline impact

 - Share results and tradeoffs with the team using a clear measurement framework

 - Work daily across our GTM stack: HubSpot, Clay, Common Room, 6sense, Salesforce, Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Scrunch, and Ahrefs, and improve workflows, automation, and data quality throughout


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5-7 years in growth, performance, or social marketing, with hands-on experience owning paid channels

 - A strong data-first, experiment-based mentality: you form hypotheses, test fast, read the data honestly, and scale only what earns it

 - An AI-first operator who uses tools like Claude and Codex to move faster, automate the manual, and scale your own output

 - Demonstrated ability to own paid performance across search and social and manage spend to pipeline and efficiency targets

 - Hands-on expertise in web CRO and earned-visibility tactics like backlinking

 - Experience owning organic and paid social with a clear point of view on what converts

 - Experience running PR in house, with strong writing and news sense

 - Analytical operator who lives in dashboards and attribution and can defend spend decisions with data

 - Hands-on with a modern GTM and analytics stack: HubSpot, Clay, Common Room, 6sense, Salesforce, Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Scrunch, and Ahrefs

 - Strong project management and communication skills


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role owns the paid engine, social presence, and PR program that bring buyers to us, convert them, and build our voice in the market. You'll set the strategy, run the experiments, and own performance, social, and PR results, working alongside a strong bench of growth peers, with room to grow scope as you prove impact.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
About the Role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Senior Accountant to join our finance team. This role will play an integral part in managing our financial processes, including revenue accounting, equity, technical accounting research, and owning our external audit process. The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who enjoys wearing multiple hats, thrives on accuracy, and has a passion for continuous learning and process improvement - particularly with Finance Leadership being one of CloudZero’s primary buyers. This person will collaborate closely with our accounting associate, with both roles reporting directly to Finance leadership.

Responsibilities:

Revenue & Cost Accounting

• Review and analyze revenue contracts in Campfire to ensure proper treatment and documentation

• Advise on and apply revenue recognition standards (ASC 606) across the business

• Record revenue entries and maintain accuracy of the revenue ledger

• Book cost of goods sold (COGS) in alignment with recognized revenue

• Review T&E entries prepared by the accounting associate, ensuring policy compliance

Equity Accounting

• Manage stock-based compensation accounting and related journal entries

• Maintain cap table and equity rollforward schedules

• Partner with legal and HR on equity-related reporting and disclosures

Technical Accounting

• Research and apply new and existing accounting standards (GAAP/ASC)

• Draft technical accounting memos and position papers as needed

• Serve as a resource for complex accounting questions across the team

Audit & Controls

• Manage the external audit process - PBC lists, coordinate auditor requests, and serve as primary point of contact

• Support internal controls documentation and testing

Process & Product Improvement

• Identify and implement operational workflow improvements across the accounting function

• Help build and document scalable accounting processes as the company grows

• CloudZero super-user and provide Finance-oriented feedback to Product and GTM teams

Qualifications:

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

• 3–5 years of relevant experience in accounting or finance

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

• Ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt to changing requirements

• Self-starter with a strong work ethic, eagerness to learn, and willingness to take on various responsibilities

• Comfortable learning and working in new technology platforms (we use Campfire, Ramp, and other modern tools)

• Experience with revenue accounting, technical accounting, or audit a plus

• CPA preferred

• Based in Boston and willing to commute to the office at least 2x per week preferred

Equal Opportunity Employer
CloudZero is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. All job offers are contingent upon the candidate passing background and reference checks.

source_jobs.title: GRC Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is growing fast. Our customer base is expanding, the regulatory and risk landscape is getting more complex, and the business needs a GRC function that can keep pace. As the GRC Manager at CloudZero, you’ll own and scale our governance, risk, and compliance programs across the organization.

Reporting to the Sr. Director of IT & Security within the Office of the CTO organization, you’ll partner closely with Legal, Engineering, Product, Sales, and G&A to build a GRC function that protects CloudZero’s interests, earns customer trust, and gives the business the confidence to move quickly.

This is a high-impact, highly cross-functional role. You’ll be as comfortable presenting a risk register to leadership as you are helping a sales team close a deal with the right compliance documentation. This is a hybrid role with an expectation of in-office presence 2–3 days per week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


DESIGN AND OPERATE THE GRC FRAMEWORK

 - Design and operate a comprehensive GRC framework spanning governance structures, enterprise risk management, and compliance programs that grows alongside CloudZero’s business

 - Own audit and certification programs including SOC 2 and other relevant standards, coordinating across internal teams and third-party auditors to drive successful outcomes

 - Own the development, maintenance, and ongoing improvement of CloudZero’s security and privacy policies and procedures, ensuring they’re current, practical, and embedded into how teams actually operate

 - Lead regular enterprise risk assessments, maintain a living risk register, and create an environment where risk-informed decision-making happens at every level of the organization




GOVERNANCE, RISK & BUSINESS CONTINUITY

 - Serve as a key stakeholder in building CloudZero’s AI Governance & Strategic Risk strategy

 - Take full ownership of business continuity and disaster recovery programs, including program design, documentation, regular testing cycles, and tabletop exercises — ensuring operational preparedness when it matters most

 - Build and manage third-party risk management processes, including vendor due diligence, contract reviews, and ongoing monitoring throughout the vendor lifecycle

 - Track regulatory developments alongside the Legal team, ensuring CloudZero meets its obligations under GDPR, CCPA, and other applicable requirements

 - Manage the company’s security awareness training program and run internal audits to validate that controls are working as intended




SALES AND REVENUE ENABLEMENT

 - Own the security questionnaire and assessment process — including VSAs, SIGs, and custom customer requests — with a primary focus on building and scaling tooling and automation that makes high-quality responses fast and repeatable

 - Review and redline security and data privacy language in customer and prospect contracts, working closely with Legal to protect CloudZero’s interests while keeping deals on track

 - Build and maintain a library of pre-approved security responses, compliance artifacts, and contract language so the team isn’t starting from scratch on every deal

 - Actively identify and implement tooling to automate questionnaire responses and security review workflows, reducing manual effort and accelerating deal cycles without sacrificing quality

 - Maintain and continuously improve CloudZero’s trust center, ensuring prospective customers have ready access to up-to-date security and compliance documentation

 - Partner with Sales Engineering and Solutions teams to address security and compliance requirements early in the sales cycle, removing friction before it becomes a blocker




WHAT YOU BRING


GOVERNANCE, RISK & COMPLIANCE

 - 5+ years of experience in governance, risk, and/or compliance roles, ideally within a SaaS or cloud technology company

 - Proven experience building or significantly maturing a GRC program, with direct, hands-on involvement in SOC 2 or similar certification audits

 - Wo

source_jobs.title: Senior IT Operations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR IT OPERATIONS ENGINEER

CloudZero is hiring a Senior IT Operations Engineer who treats IT as a product instead of a help desk. You will work with our IT leadership to keep our internal systems quiet, fast, and secure. You will also lean on AI every day to do it.

The role is hybrid out of our Boston office. Some flexibility outside East Coast hours is helpful, given our employees and customers are global.




OUR OPERATING PHILOSOPHY

We measure success by high-quality outcomes, not volume; not by tickets closed, but by tickets that never get opened.

Every helpdesk ticket that hits our support tool, is a signal. Something about a system, a workflow, or a piece of documentation failed a person. The job is to look under the signal, ask why the question came up in the first place, and fix the upstream cause so the next ten people don't run into the same wall.

That means:

• When a ticket lands, your first instinct should be "what would make this ticket not exist next week?"

• You will build automations, self-service paths, and documentation that retire whole categories of tickets at a time.

• Patterns in our helpdesk data should shape your roadmap. You will spot recurring asks, group them by root cause, and chip away at them.

• A great week ends with fewer open issues and fewer total inbound tickets than the week before.



We're AI-native, for real

We use AI tooling every day, and the right person for this opportunity has an inquisitive mindset and has already ingrained this new way of working with strong human judgment.

• You reach for Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or ChatGPT before problem-solving manually.

• You use AI to draft scripts, parse logs, summarize vendor docs, write runbooks, and break apart messy projects.

• You can talk credibly about which AI tools are good at what, where they fall over, and how to prompt them well.

• You try new AI tools as they show up, and you drop them when they don't earn their keep.

If writing a prompt is your default move when something looks unfamiliar, you will fit in.



What the role looks like day-to-day

• Own and maintain the core systems the company runs on (JAMF, Okta, Slack, Jira, Google Workspace)

• Build automations in Okta Workflows or from scratch that quietly handle work that used to be tickets.

• Write Python and Bash that can connect to our SaaS tools together.

• Treat the Helpdesk queue as a data source. Analyze it, categorize it, and root-cause aggressively.

• Partner with Security so new tooling is safe by default, not safe by review.

• Provide responsive, human first-touch support when issues reach your queue, then address the root cause to prevent repeat requests.

• Drive cross-functional IT projects from intake through rollout, with stakeholders bought in along the way.

• Partner with the People team to ensure a smooth employee lifecycle experience



What we’re looking for

• 5+ years running and configuring real IT or cloud infrastructure at a growing company.

• 2+ years of hands-on automation work in Workato, Okta Workflows, Python, Bash, or similar.

• Real AI fluency. You have used modern AI tools to ship actual work, not just an experiment.

• Familiarity with the IT toolkit: JAMF (including packaging and deployment), Okta SSO, Slack, Jira, Google Workspace.

• 2+ years of operational time inside GCP, Snowflake, and/or AWS.

• Strong API instincts. You have stitched SaaS tools together with webhooks, REST APIs, and event hooks.

• A root-cause mindset. You are not satisfied with closing a ticket without knowing why it appeared.

• A bias for shipping. You can take a vague request and produce a clean outcome.

• Real partnership instincts with security, not box-checking.

• You are exceptional with people and have a customer-first mindset. First-touch support is part of the job, and how you make someone feel matters.

• Experience automating employee lifecycle (joiners, movers, leavers), including directly integrating HRIS systems.


Ni

source_jobs.title: Senior Executive Business Partner
source_jobs.description:
Senior Executive Business Partner, Office of the CEO

 - Reports to: VP of People & Culture

 - Supports: CEO, Executive Chairman, Founder/CTO, Chief Product Officer, SVP of Customer Success, and broader support for ELT

 - Location: Boston (3 days/week in office)

About CloudZero

CloudZero is the AI ROI Company. We help the world's most ambitious companies understand whether their cloud and AI investments are actually working — and we're defining a category before it has a name. CFOs are turning to us to answer one of the most important business questions of this decade, and we're scaling fast to meet the moment.

About the Role

The office of the CEO is where the most important work at CloudZero gets coordinated, decided, and driven forward. As we scale into a new category, that office needs to operate with precision, pace, and care.

This is a Senior Executive Business Partner role for someone who treats that as a craft.

This is not a traditional EA role. You'll be a trusted operational partner to our most senior leaders and the connective tissue across the executive team. You'll own complex calendars, but you'll also own executive meeting rhythm, offsite and onsite design, and the cross-functional projects that move faster because you're driving them. You'll anticipate what our executives need before they ask. You'll make every interaction with their offices feel sharp, prepared, and intentional. And you'll build the operating systems that scale the executive function as the company grows.

You'll thrive here if you treat coordination as a craft, take pride in flawless execution, hold a high bar for everyone around you, and want to build memorable experiences and lasting relationships with internal and external partners.

What You'll Own

 - Strategic priorities and operating system: Partner with the CEO and ELT to track quarterly priorities, OKRs, and the strategic initiatives that matter most. Surface what's drifting, escalate what's stuck, and keep the leadership team focused on the work that moves the company forward.

 - Executive Leadership Team rhythm and decision quality: Own the weekly ELT meeting end-to-end: agenda design, pre-reads, decision capture, action items tracked to close. Build the operating cadence that keeps our leadership team aligned, productive, and accountable. Sharpen the quality of decisions by structuring tradeoffs, framing options, and following through on what gets agreed.

 - Strategic initiatives and cross-functional execution: Drive the initiatives that sit between functions and would otherwise stall. Lead projects on behalf of the CEO when they need a trusted owner who isn't a function head. You won't always be the subject matter expert; you'll be the operator who makes sure decisions get made, owners are clear, and work ships on time.

 - Executive communications and information flow: Draft, review, and synthesize the communications that move in and out of the CEO's office. Brief executives ahead of key meetings. Capture takeaways. Make sure the right information reaches the right people at the right time.

 - Board and investor coordination: Partner with the CEO on board meeting prep, materials coordination, scheduling, and follow-through. Help shape board content, synthesize what matters, and serve as a confident, polished front door for board members and investors.

 - Offsites, onsites, All Hands, and the company retreat: Design and execute the moments that shape how this company works together. Venue, agenda, content flow, vendor management, logistics, and the small details that turn a meeting into an experience people remember.

 - Executive operations across the office of the CEO: Manage complex, fast-moving calendars across the CEO, Executive Chairman, CTO, CPO, and SVP of Customer Success. Make tradeoff calls in real time. Protect deep work, board prep, customer time, and strategic thinking with equal rigor.

 - Travel and expense management: Build itineraries that work. Proce

source_jobs.title: Staff / Principal Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is hiring Staff and Principal Software Engineers across our engineering organization. We're not filling a single seat. We're building a bench of senior technical talent across every team that makes CloudZero run.

If you're the kind of engineer who wants to work on hard problems at real scale, own architecture decisions that matter, and see your work land directly in the hands of customers and operators, there's a place for you here.

These roles sit at the intersection of technical depth and product impact. You'll be expected to lead from the front: designing systems, shaping roadmaps, mentoring engineers, and driving decisions that outlast any single sprint. The specific domain depends on where you land, but the bar and the opportunity are consistent across the org.

 


WHERE YOU MIGHT WORK

Agent Platform This team is the spearhead of CloudZero's push into AI cost intelligence. You'll work on the AI telemetry agent that captures what coding agents and AI workloads actually cost at the developer endpoint: token usage, cost per workflow, latency, and where the value goes. You'll design the data model, ship the agent, and work directly with product on what to expose to the customer. macOS first; other endpoints to follow. A second surface on this team is our Kubernetes metric agent, already in production across customer-controlled clusters, where you're solving for resource constraints you don't set and security postures you don't control.

Billing Ingest This team owns the architecture at the heart of the product: the backend data processing, storage, and query infrastructure that everything else depends on. You'll design data models, maintain and enhance a custom-built query engine, and build for scale as we onboard customers with increasingly large datasets and complex analytical requirements. If you want to work on the pipeline that processed 14 trillion billing events in the last twelve months, this is where you do it.

Platform Services and Applications This team owns what customers touch every day: cost intelligence features like dimensions, CostFormation, and Explorer UI, as well as the identity and authentication layer including SSO, RBAC, and multi-tenancy for enterprise customers. When you ship a feature here, customers use it immediately and you hear about it. No layers of abstraction between your work and user value.

Integrations This team builds and maintains all billing connectors across every provider CloudZero supports, and is actively expanding into AI data ingestion, building the pipelines that handle new sources of AI telemetry for customers running AI-forward workloads. If you want breadth of system surface and a fast-moving target, this is it.

Cost Optimization This team owns some of CloudZero's most customer-visible product features. That includes Optimize, which the team fully overhauled and rebuilt in FY2026, and Anomaly Detection, which is being rebuilt around a new AI-driven detection engine. The team also owns Ticket Integrations, Notifications, Views, and Budgets. If you want to work at the product surface where engineering decisions translate directly into customer outcomes, and where AI is actively reshaping what the feature set looks like, this team is doing that work now.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own significant technical domains end to end: architecture, implementation, and the long-term health of the systems you build

 - Make and communicate high-impact design decisions across teams and stakeholders

 - Identify and drive improvements to performance, scalability, and reliability before they become incidents

 - Collaborate with product, engineering leadership, and customers to scope problems and deliver solutions that move the business

 - Raise the technical bar for the engineers around you through code review, design feedback, and direct mentorship

 - Operate with a high degree of autonomy; you don't need a ticket to identify a problem worth solving

 
 


WH

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff CloudOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

CloudZero is growing fast. Our customer base is expanding, the data challenges we're solving are getting more complex, and the platform is scaling to match. As a CloudOps Engineer you'll be a force multiplier for our engineering organization, owning the performance, reliability, and observability of CloudZero's infrastructure and empowering teams to ship features that help customers understand and optimize their cloud spend.

This is real infrastructure work at real scale, not a ticket-closing role or a console-clicking job. CloudZero processes billions of events daily across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Our customers rely on real-time, accurate cost data to make business-critical decisions, and any instability in our system impacts their planning. Built entirely on a unique serverless architecture with no EC2s or containers, our platform demands infrastructure that scales gracefully, fails predictably, and recovers automatically.

If you thrive on hard operational problems, care deeply about reliability and performance, and want to see your work matter to customers in direct and measurable ways, this role was built for you.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Infrastructure as Code

 - Design and maintain Pulumi modules that provision reliable, cost-efficient cloud resources

 - Own infrastructure end to end with no clicking through consoles

Observability

 - Instrument systems so that failures surface quickly and debugging happens with data, not guesswork

 - Build observability into everything so you know about problems before customers do

Automation

 - Automate deployments, scaling, backups, and limit changes; if humans are doing it repeatedly, build a system to do it instead

 - Balance automation intelligently, building solutions to real problems rather than automating for its own sake

Partner with Product Engineering

 - Help teams design resilient services, review architectures for operational complexity, and build deployment pipelines that enable safe and fast shipping

 - Optimize for cost and performance; CloudZero's business is helping others optimize cloud costs, and we should be exemplars of efficient cloud usage ourselves


WHAT YOU BRING

 - 3 to 5+ years of experience building and operating distributed systems in AWS

 - Strong skills in Python and Infrastructure as Code using Pulumi or Terraform

 - Experience with frontier AI models such as Claude, Codex, or Gemini

 - Hands-on experience with monitoring tools such as Prometheus or Datadog

 - Proven ability to debug production issues under pressure

 - Values thoughtful, reliable system design over reactive hero efforts

 - Strong documentation habits to support long-term team clarity and system stability

 - Ability to clearly explain complex technical issues to non-technical stakeholders

 - Excited to take ownership of infrastructure and solve operational challenges at scale


ABOUT CLOUDZERO

Cloud cost management is one of the biggest challenges organizations face today. As cloud adoption continues to accelerate, so do the complexities and costs associated with it, and macroeconomic conditions only increase pressure to prove cloud efficiency.

CloudZero is a SaaS platform at the intersection of next-generation cloud cost management and FinOps. We ingest billing and usage data from all cloud, SaaS, and PaaS providers, organize it in real time according to our customers' business structures, and empower organizations to make more informed business decisions.

Since our founding in 2016, our mission has been to make efficient innovation a reality for every cloud-driven organization. We believe every engineering decision is a buying decision, and we're applying proven reliability engineering principles to financial efficiency.

We believe the best AI empowers users with clear insights and confident decisions, transforming complex cloud cost data into actionable intelligence that drives meaningful business outcomes.

To date, we've raised over $56 million from l

notes: High-growth SaaS company with strong engineering focus, AI-native product, and expanding customer base.
